script.arcTitle: South Africa v Canada: The Late Breakthrough
script.openingLine: At Los Angeles Stadium, South Africa meet Canada in the round of 32.

moments.spokenLine: South Africa meet Canada in the round of 32 at Los Angeles Stadium. The replay is under way.
moments.explainer: The match begins with both sides level at the start of the round of 32.

moments.spokenLine: Neither side has broken through: South Africa 0–0 Canada. The contest remains finely balanced.
moments.explainer: The score is still level at the interval, with no goal separating the teams.

moments.spokenLine: Nathan SALIBA is shown a card for Canada, adding a moment of tension to this scoreless match.
moments.explainer: Canada receive the first recorded yellow card while the score remains level.

moments.spokenLine: Niko SIGUR is shown a card for Canada, another flash of discipline in a scoreless contest.
moments.explainer: Canada receive a second recorded yellow card before either side has scored.

moments.spokenLine: Stephen EUSTAQUIO scores for Canada, and the verified score moves to 0–1. The match has its decisive swing.
moments.explainer: Canada move in front late on, turning a level match into a one-goal contest.

moments.spokenLine: South Africa 0–1 Canada. Stephen EUSTAQUIO’s goal is the difference, and Canada go through.
moments.explainer: The final score confirms Canada’s victory by the only goal of the match.

chapters.text: At kick-off, South Africa meet Canada in the round of 32 at Los Angeles Stadium. The replay is under way.
At half-time, Neither side has broken through: South Africa 0–0 Canada. The contest remains finely balanced.
In the 54th minute, Nathan SALIBA is shown a card for Canada, adding a moment of tension to this scoreless match.
In the 67th minute, Niko SIGUR is shown a card for Canada, another flash of discipline in a scoreless contest.
In the 2nd minute of second-half stoppage time, Stephen EUSTAQUIO scores for Canada, and the verified score moves to 0–1. The match has its decisive swing.
At full-time, South Africa 0–1 Canada. Stephen EUSTAQUIO’s goal is the difference, and Canada go through.
